title: dodonaea lanceolata var. lanceolata
intro: Dodonaea lanceolata var. lanceolata, also known as the Horse Sage Bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to Western Australia and commonly found in coastal areas. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its leaves, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dodonaea lanceolata var. lanceolata include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as an expectorant, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve the leaves being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
